6:
SELECT
    (
        (SUM(CASE WHEN Segment = 'SME' AND Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID, Date LIKE '2013%' AND Segment = 'SME')) -
        (SUM(CASE WHEN Segment = 'LAM' AND Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID, Date LIKE '2013%' AND Segment = 'LAM'))
    ) AS SME_LAM_difference,
    (
        (SUM(CASE WHEN Segment = 'LAM' AND Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID, Date LIKE '2013%' AND Segment = 'LAM')) -
        (SUM(CASE WHEN Segment = 'KAM' AND Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID, Date LIKE '2013%' AND Segment = 'KAM'))
    ) AS LAM_KAM_difference,
    (
        (SUM(CASE WHEN Segment = 'KAM' AND Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID, Date LIKE '2013%' AND Segment = 'KAM')) -
        (SUM(CASE WHEN Segment = 'SME' AND Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID, Date LIKE '2013%' AND Segment = 'SME'))
    ) AS KAM_SME_difference
FROM yearmonth
GROUP BY Segment;	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing
7:
SELECT Segment, AVG(Consumption) as Avg_Consumption_2012, AVG(Consumption) as Avg_Consumption_2013,
 (AVG(Consumption) for 2013 - AVG(Consumption) for 2012) as Increase_or_Decrease,
 ( (AVG(Consumption) for 2013 - AVG(Consumption) for 2012) / AVG(Consumption) for 2013 ) * 100% as Percentage_of_Increase
FROM (
    SELECT CustomerID, Date, Consumption
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE Date LIKE '2012%'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT CustomerID, Date, Consumption
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE Date LIKE '2013%'
) as Consumption_Data
GROUP BY Segment
ORDER BY Percentage_of_Increase DESC, Avg_Consumption_2013 DESC
LIMIT 3;


This SQL code calculates the average consumption for each segment in 2012 and 2013, the increase or decrease between the two years, and the percentage of increase. It then groups the results by segment and orders them by the percentage of increase in descending order and the average consumption in 2013 in descending order. Finally, it limits the results to the top 3 segments.	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

This SQL statement calculates the average amount of loans that are still on running contract (status = 'C') and have been issued before each transaction (issued <= date of the transaction with type 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU'). The result is the average amount of such loans.	----- bird -----	financial
497:
SELECT client.client_id, client.birth_date
FROM client
JOIN disp ON client.district_id = disp.district_id
JOIN card ON disp.account_id = card.card_id
WHERE card.type = 'gold' AND disp.type = 'OWNER'	----- bird -----	financial
498:
SELECT account_id, district.A2 AS district_name, district.A3 AS district_region
FROM account
JOIN disp ON account.account_id = disp.account_id
JOIN client ON disp.client_id = client.client_id
JOIN district ON client.district_id = district.district_id
JOIN trans ON account.account_id = trans.account_id
WHERE trans.type = 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' AND trans.date <= '1993-12-31' AND trans.date > '1993-01-01';	----- bird -----	financial
499:
SELECT account_id, frequency
FROM account
INNER JOIN district ON account.district_id = district.district_id
WHERE district.A2 = 'east Bohemia' AND date BETWEEN '1995-01-01' AND '2000-12-31'	----- bird -----	financial
